title: Pseudopodospermum turcomanicum
taxonomy: Pseudopodospermum turcomanicum is a species of flowering plant in the family Celastraceae, which includes the bittersweet nightshade and spindletree. It is native to Central Asia. The classification of Pseudopodospermum turcomanicum is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, and the order Celastrales. This plant's classification is also represented by its binomial nomenclature, with Pseudopodospermum turcomanicum being its scientific name. The specific epithet 'turcomanicum' refers to its origin in Turkmenistan.

The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can aid in relieving symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and muscle spasms.
